refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the request about?
refinementEntries.description: Report issues with bicycle signals (wait time is too long, green is too short). Request a bicycle green ahead of motor vehicles, a new bicycle signal or report a bicycle signal stuck or bike signal push button issue.

refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the request about?
refinementEntries.description: Request for maintenance of a damaged flashing beacon or damaged school safety zone flashing beacon.

refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the request about?
refinementEntries.description: Report pedestrian signal issues such as excessive wait time, not enough time to cross, signals or signal buttons that may be stuck, not working properly, or accessible pedestrian signals that are too loud/soft.

refinementEntries.optionLabel: School Flashing Beacon Damaged or Missing
refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the request about?
refinementEntries.description: Report a missing or damaged school safety zone flashing beacon. Damage can include: beacon is twisted, facing the wrong way, cable low, loose or exposed, constantly on, not flashing or out, pole is down or leaning, etc.

refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the request about?
refinementEntries.description: Request traffic signal (vehicle, pedestrian, transit & bicycle) information, such as timing reports/cards, programming sheets, drawings, synchro models, analysis reports related to signals.

refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the request about?
refinementEntries.description: Request repair of a malfunctioning or damaged traffic signal, signal box, audible crosswalk, or illuminated sign.

refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the request about?
refinementEntries.description: Report issues with transit signals such as transit signal phase being stuck, on recall, too long, too short or not working; transit detector failure; excessive delays to transit vehicles, Transit Signal Priority (TSP) not working, new TSP request, etc.

refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the request about?
refinementEntries.description: Report issues with traffic signals such as signal coordination, excessive vehicle delays, long wait time, access blocking, left-turn/right-turn phase, confusing signal display, congestion due to construction/special events, red light running, etc.

refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the request about?
refinementEntries.description: Request investigation into the installation, modification or removal of a regulatory sign such as Lane Designation, Turn Prohibitions, Buses/Bicycle excepted

refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the request about?
refinementEntries.description: Request an investigation for the installation of a temporary sign or to investigate an existing temporary sign. Types of requests could be lack of signage about road work or construction, detour signs, or removal of temporary sign.

refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the request about?
refinementEntries.description: Request an investigation for the need for installation, removal or modification of warning signs such as intersection warning, curve warning, pedestrian warning, lane ends, playgroud ahead, etc. on a City of Toronto road.

refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the request about?
refinementEntries.description: Report a missing or damaged In-Road Flexible Sign on the road.

refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the request about?
refinementEntries.description: Report a missing or damaged Watch Your Speed board.

refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the request about?
refinementEntries.description: Report a missing, damaged or faded emergency traffic sign.

refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the request about?

refinementEntries.parentProblemTypeName: Request Water Service Turn-On or Off
refinementEntries.optionLabel: Emergency Water Service Turn Off due to Pipe Leak or Burst
refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is this request about?
refinementEntries.description: Request an emergency water service turn off.
path: Request Water Service Turn-On or Off

refinementEntries.parentProblemTypeName: Request Water Service Turn-On or Off
refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is this request about?
refinementEntries.description: Request for a first-time water service turn-on for a building that is newly built and has a 4-inch or larger water service (e.g. townhouse complexes, multi-story residential, commercial or industrial buildings).
path: Request Water Service Turn-On or Off

refinementEntries.parentProblemTypeName: Request Water Service Turn-On or Off
refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is this request about?
refinementEntries.description: Book an appointment to turn off the water service between 8 a.m. to 8 p.m., Monday to Sunday. Book a water service turn-on as well, and the water turn-on fee will be waived if it is within 30 minutes of water turn-off, and the staff is still on-site.
path: Request Water Service Turn-On or Off

refinementEntries.parentProblemTypeName: Request Water Service Turn-On or Off
refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is this request about?
refinementEntries.description: Book an appointment to turn on the water service. Appointments are available 24 hours a day, 7 days a week. The water turn-on fee will be waived if it is within 30 minutes of water turn off, and the staff is still on-site.
path: Request Water Service Turn-On or Off

refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the concern you are reporting?

refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the issue you are reporting?
refinementEntries.description: Report maintenance holes that are damaged or require maintenance for grates that are blocked, flooding hazard or is a potential safety hazard for pedestrians or cyclists.

refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the issue you are reporting?
refinementEntries.description: Request for the repair or replacement of a manhole/maintenance cover.

refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the concern you are reporting?
refinementEntries.description: Request investigation and repair of a damaged retaining wall on City property.

refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the concern you are reporting?
refinementEntries.description: Request road cleaning or removing debris or litter on a City roadway

refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the concern you are reporting?
refinementEntries.description: Report damage (such as holes, cracks) to a gravel road in the City of Toronto.

refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the concern you are reporting?
refinementEntries.description: Report pothole or road damage or uneven pavement on a City of Toronto roadway.

refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the concern you are reporting?
refinementEntries.description: Request repair of a road that is sinking or has settled. Visible signs may include ponding of water or depressions due to a water leak or sub-surface settlement on the road.

refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the concern you are reporting?
refinementEntries.description: Request inspection of utility cut repair that is loose and/or making noise. Utility cuts can be identified by rectangular asphalt patches on the road or roadside. Note: Metal plates are often temporarily installed over utility cuts.

refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the concern you are reporting?
refinementEntries.description: Request inspection of utility cut repair on the road or roadside. Utility cuts can be identified by rectangular asphalt patches on the road or roadside. Note: metal plates are often temporarily installed over utility cuts.

refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the concern you are reporting?
refinementEntries.description: Report pothole or road damage or uneven pavement on a City of Toronto roadway.

refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the concern you are reporting?
refinementEntries.description: Request repair and maintenance of damaged shoulder on a City road.

refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: Where is the issue?
refinementEntries.description: Report ponding or standing water on the roadway or roadside.

refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: Where is the issue?
refinementEntries.description: Request inspection and repair of a sidewalk with standing or ponding water.

refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the concern you are reporting?
refinementEntries.description: Report a damaged traffic island or traffic island handrails.

refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: Is the noise from a moving motor vehicle?
refinementEntries.description: Report audible noise from moving motor vehicles, including those with loud exhaust, squealing tires, horns, engine revs, or rapid acceleration.

refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: Is the noise from a moving motor vehicle?
refinementEntries.description: Report an audible noise from stationary motor vehicles, such as the sounding of a horn, revving of an engine, or any like sound and/or stationary vehicle repairs, rebuilding, modifying, or testing outside permitted hours.

refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: Is this a request to investigate an aggressive dog that is owned or stray?
refinementEntries.description: Report an aggressive owned dog (e.g., not stray) that is exhibiting behaviours that pose a menace to the safety of persons or domestic animals.

refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: Is this a request to investigate an aggressive dog that is owned or stray?
refinementEntries.description: Report an aggressive stray dog exhibiting behaviours that pose a menace to the safety of persons or domestic animals.

refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the issue that you are reporting?
refinementEntries.description: Report an animal owner who fails to provide adequate care for their animals. Adequate and appropriate care includes care sufficient to preserve the health and well-being of an animal (for example not providing food or water).

refinementEntries.optionLabel: Animal kept outside in extreme weather
refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the issue that you are reporting?
refinementEntries.description: Report a dog owner who fails to provide adequate care for the dog or the dog is in a potentially serious state, e.g. starvation, dehydration, inadequate shelter, etc.

refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the issue with the dog?
refinementEntries.description: Report a dog who is kept on a chain, rope or similar restraining device of less than two (2) meters in length, with restricted movement and suffering. The dog shall not be tethered unsupervised for longer than one hour.

refinementEntries.optionLabel: Tied for more than one hour
refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the issue with the dog?
refinementEntries.description: Report a dog that is being tied and left unsupervised from its owner for longer than one hour. Note: If animal cruelty is involved, Toronto Animal Services will contact Toronto Police.

refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the issue with the dog?
refinementEntries.description: Report a person who is keeping a dog on a choke collar, choke chain, pronged collar or any similar device that is harmful to the animal. Note: If animal cruelty is involved, Toronto Animal Services will contact Toronto Police directly.

refinementEntries.optionLabel: Commercial dog walker with more than 6 dogs and/or without licence in a park
refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the issue that you are reporting?
refinementEntries.description: Report a commercial dog walker who is walking more than six dogs in a City park, green space, waterfront area without the permit visibly displayed.

refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the issue that you are reporting?
refinementEntries.description: Report incidents of dogs harassing or chasing coyotes.

refinementEntries.optionLabel: Dog being walked without a leash
refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the complaint about?
refinementEntries.description: Report an owner whose dog is off-leash outside of a designated off-leash area.

refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the complaint about?
refinementEntries.description: Report a dog allowed to frequently run at large or allowed to roam (owner not present). All dogs must be in care and control of a person and on a leash (unless in designated off-leash areas).

refinementEntries.problemTypeName: Dog Off-Leash in a City Park
refinementEntries.optionLabel: Dog off-leash in a park with the owner
refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the complaint about?
refinementEntries.description: Report a dog off-leash and not under the care or control of a person in a City park outside of a designated off-leash area.
path: Dog Off-Leash in a City Park

refinementEntries.optionLabel: Dog off-leash in a park without the owner
refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the complaint about?
refinementEntries.description: Report a stray domestic animal off the premises of the owner and not in care and control of a person. Toronto Animal Services requests if possible to confine the animal or remain with the animal until an officer arrives.

refinementEntries.optionLabel: Dog off-leash on a school property
refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the complaint about?
refinementEntries.description: Report dog off-leash incidents on school property.

refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the complaint about?
refinementEntries.description: Report a dog owner with a dangerous dog designation that fails to muzzle the dog.

refinementEntries.problemTypeName: Investigate - Dangerous Dog in Off-Leash Area
refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the complaint about?
refinementEntries.description: Report a dog owner who allows a dog with a dangerous dog designation to use public off-leash areas
path: Investigate - Dangerous Dog in Off-Leash Area

refinementEntries.problemTypeName: Investigate - Dangerous Dog with no Warning Sign Displayed
refinementEntries.optionLabel: Dangerous Dog with no Warning Sign Displayed
refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the complaint about?
refinementEntries.description: Report a dog owner who fails to display the required dangerous dog warning sign on their property
path: Investigate - Dangerous Dog with no Warning Sign Displayed

refinementEntries.problemTypeName: Investigate - Dangerous Dog with no Dangerous Dog Tag
refinementEntries.optionLabel: Dangerous Dog with no Dangerous Dog Tag
refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the complaint about?
refinementEntries.description: Report a dog owner who fails to put the required dangerous dog tag on their dog
path: Investigate - Dangerous Dog with no Dangerous Dog Tag

refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the issue that you are reporting?
refinementEntries.description: Report a domestic animal (except a cat) that is kept outside for any continuous period exceeding thirty (30) minutes without a weather-proofed and insulated enclosure/shelter.

refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the issue that you are reporting?
refinementEntries.description: Report someone who has more than dogs three (3) or cats six (6) or rabbits (4) or guinea pigs (4) in and about a dwelling unit. Note: This does not apply to individual members of a rescue group, registered with Toronto Animal Services.

refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the issue that you are reporting?
refinementEntries.description: Report a domestic animal on private property that is living in unsanitary conditions (accumulation of animal feces or strong odour).

refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: Where is the issue?
refinementEntries.description: Report a dog owner or a dog walker failing to pick up dog excrement in a City of Toronto park.

refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: Where is the issue?
refinementEntries.description: Report a dog owner or walker who fails to pick up or remove excrement from public or private property. The address of the dog owner must be provided to complete this request. Note: For multi-residential property speak with the Property Manager.

refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the issue that you are reporting?
refinementEntries.description: Report a Pit Bull or non-compliance of the Dog Owners Liability Act (DOLA) by the owner.

refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the issue that you are reporting?
refinementEntries.description: Report a person who is using a prohibited collar (choke chain/collar) or keeping a dog on a rope/similar restraining device of less than three (3 meters) in length. Note: If animal cruelty is involved, Toronto Animal Services will contact Toronto Police.

refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the issue that you are reporting?
refinementEntries.description: Submit a service request for an investigation into an unlicensed cat or dog. All dogs and cats owned in Toronto must be licensed and wear a tag.

refinementEntries.optionLabel: Walking more than 3 dogs on City property
refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the issue that you are reporting?
refinementEntries.description: Report a person who is walking more than three dogs anywhere in the City without a commercial dog walker's permit. The permit must be prominently displayed. Note: Commercial dog walkers are allowed to walk 4-6 dogs at a time.

refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the issue that you are reporting?
refinementEntries.description: Request for pick-up of abandoned shopping cart on the City road allowance.

refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the issue that you are reporting?
refinementEntries.description: Report damaged asphalt on a City of Toronto boulevard.

refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: What is the issue that you are reporting?
refinementEntries.description: Report a street furniture maintenance concern, graffiti, damage or request the installation, relocation of a street furniture element (transit shelters, benches, litter bins, publication box/corral, info pillar etc.) on the public right-of-way.

refinementEntries.sourceQuestionText: Is the walkway between the street and a City park?
refinementEntries.description: Request walkway ice or snow clearing.

eligibilityQuestions.questionText: Who is making this complaint?

picklistOptions.label: Owner of the property
picklistOptions.ineligibleExplanationText: Property owners are responsible for the maintenance of their own property.

eligibilityQuestions.questionText: Are you reporting that a property is not meeting the minimum standards prescribed by the Toronto Municipal Code (City By-Law) on maintenance and occupancy?

picklistOptions.label: No
picklistOptions.ineligibleExplanationText: This service request is for reporting a property that does not meet the City's municipal code rules on maintenance and occupancy.  Please review the knowledge base to determine the appropriate request.

eligibilityQuestions.questionText: Have you formally notified the landlord/owner in writing, but the problem has not been resolved for more than 7 days?

picklistOptions.label: No
picklistOptions.ineligibleExplanationText: We recommend  non tenants/tenants speak with the property owner/manager first to try an resolve the issue.  If it cannot be solved this way, please complete a service request.

eligibilityQuestions.helpText: Examples: residential renovation, landscaping, and construction of a single family dwelling home.

data.description: Report a property that does not meet the City's municipal code or rules on property maintenance and occupancy, such as pest control, retaining walls, vacant buildings, roofs, stairs, elevator, ventilation, dust from residential construction, clothing box.

learnMoreArticle.title: Sick, Injured or Distressed Animals in Toronto

intakeQuestions.questionText: Are you reporting this between 1:00 a.m. - 6:00 a.m.?
intakeQuestions.helpText: Toronto Animal Services staff are unavailable from 1:00 to 6:00 a.m. Service requests submitted during this time will be reviewed after 6:00 a.m.

intakeQuestions.questionText: Indicate animal Type

intakeQuestions.regExValidationMessage: Only alphanumeric characters, space, parentheses, strudels, commas, apostrophes, ampersand, forward slash, question mark, hyphens, colon, and periods are allowed.
intakeQuestions.questionText: Description of animal (Species, Type or Colour)

intakeQuestions.regExValidationMessage: Only alphanumeric characters, space, parentheses, strudels, commas, apostrophes, ampersand, forward slash, question mark, hyphens, colon, and periods are allowed.
intakeQuestions.questionText: Details of the location of the injured animal such as landmark, direction, specific address, etc.

intakeQuestions.regExValidationMessage: Only alphanumeric characters, space, parentheses, strudels, commas, apostrophes, ampersand, forward slash, question mark, hyphens, colon, and periods are allowed.
intakeQuestions.questionText: Details of the injury such as visible injuries (bleeding, deformity), cause of injury, location on body, severity, and observed symptoms (such as limping, staggering, paralyzed hind legs and acting confused or attacking non-living objects).
Is the animal mobile or immobile?
Is the animal confined?

eligibilityQuestions.questionText: Are you reporting injured, sick, or distressed wild animals such as raccoons, deer, birds, squirrels and foxes? Please note, Animal Services does NOT service animals that are commonly considered pests such as rats or mice.

picklistOptions.label: No
picklistOptions.ineligibleExplanationText: This service request is only for reporting sick, injured or distressed wildlife. If you wish to report a sick, injured or distressed domestic animal, please select the service request specifically for domestic animals.

eligibilityQuestions.questionText: If the animal is located on private property, someone 18 or over must be on-site to give permission for an officer to enter the property or an email giving permission should be sent to animalservices@toronto.ca

picklistOptions.label: The animal is not on private property
I or someone over 18 will remain on site
I will not be on site, but I will send an email giving permission to enter the property
I will not be on-site and I will not send an email
picklistOptions.ineligibleExplanationText: Toronto Animal Services will not be able to respond to this request.

picklistOptions.label: Yes

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription:

picklistOptions.label: Garbage Front & Side pick-up

picklistOptions.label: Hazardous Waste pick-up

picklistOptions.label: Oversized or Electronic Items - Night pick-up

eligibilityQuestions.questionText: Select the type of waste issue on private property

picklistOptions.label: Accumulation of waste or litter in a yard area
Illegal dumping that was not witnessed
picklistOptions.ineligibleExplanationText: Illegal dumping that has not been witnessed can't be investigated.  Property owners are responsible for the removal of dumped items.

picklistOptions.label: Standing water in an unused swimming pool
Standing water in drains, depressions or eavestroughs
Standing water in a fountain, bird bath or pool cover
picklistOptions.ineligibleExplanationText: Standing water in pool covers or manmade containers such as bird baths or fountains are investigated by Toronto Public Health.  Please contact Toronto Health Connection at 416-338-7600.

eligibilityQuestions.questionText: If you are a tenant, have you spoken to the landlord about the concern?

picklistOptions.label: No
picklistOptions.ineligibleExplanationText: Please contact your landlord or property manager first. If the situation is not resolved within a reasonable period of time, please submit this service request.

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription: Report human waste (solid or liquid) on the sidewalk, boulevard or in a City laneway.
picklistOptions.label: Clean up Biohazard Human Waste

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription: Request road cleaning or removing debris or litter on a City roadway
picklistOptions.label: Clean up Construction Material Spill on a Road

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription: Request road cleaning or removing debris or litter on a City roadway
picklistOptions.label: Clean up Debris or Litter on Roadways (Road and Street)

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeName: Clean up Illegal Dumping on City Road Allowance
picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription: Request to clean up items that have been illegally dumped on City road allowances such as laneways, sidewalks, and boulevards. These can be items such as multiple waste bags, oversized items, toilets, or sinks.
picklistOptions.label: Clean up Illegal Dumping on City Road Allowance (Boulevards, Sidewalks and Laneways)

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeName: Clean up Illegal Dumping on Roadways and Bridge Underpass
picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription: Request to clean up items that have been illegally dumped on the City's roadways and bridge underpass such as black bags, large quantities of garbage, and furniture. This illegal dumping was not witnessed and does not have hazardous items.
picklistOptions.label: Clean up Illegal Dumping on City Roadways (Road, Street) and Bridge Underpass

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription: Request to clean up litter and waste left on the road, sidewalk and boulevard as a result of a special event on public property.
picklistOptions.label: Clean up Litter after a Special Event

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription:
Request to clean up litter or loose garbage on public laneways.
Note: Litter includes small items or discarded items, such as paper, flyers, wrappers, cigarette butts, and broken glass.
picklistOptions.label: Clean up Litter on Laneway

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeName: Clean up Litter on Sidewalks and Boulevards
picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription:
Report litter or loose garbage on sidewalks or boulevards along City's main roads. 
Note: Litter includes small items or discarded items such as papers, flyers, wrappers, cigarette butts, and broken glass; not chewing gum.
picklistOptions.label: Clean up Litter on Sidewalk and Boulevard

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription: Report needles/syringes that have been discarded on the road, sidewalk, boulevard or in City laneways.
picklistOptions.label: Clean up Needles or Syringes

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription: Report a street furniture maintenance concern, graffiti, damage or request the installation, relocation of a street furniture element (transit shelters, benches, litter bins, publication box/corral, info pillar etc.) on the public right-of-way.
picklistOptions.label: Clean up of Litter or Debris - Street Furniture

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeName: Clean up Overflowing Street Litter Bin
picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription: Report an overflowing street litter bin on the street allowance.
picklistOptions.label: Clean up Overflowing Street Litter Bin

data.problemTypeName: Garbage, Debris and Needles on the City Road Allowance

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription: Report issues with bicycle signals (wait time is too long, green is too short). Request a bicycle green ahead of motor vehicles, a new bicycle signal or report a bicycle signal stuck or bike signal push button issue.
picklistOptions.label: Bicycle Signal Issue

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription: Request for maintenance of a damaged flashing beacon or damaged school safety zone flashing beacon.
picklistOptions.label: Flashing Beacon Maintenance

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription: Report pedestrian signal issues such as excessive wait time, not enough time to cross, signals or signal buttons that may be stuck, not working properly, or accessible pedestrian signals that are too loud/soft.
picklistOptions.label: Pedestrian Signal Issue

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription: Report a missing or damaged school safety zone flashing beacon. Damage can include: beacon is twisted, facing the wrong way, cable low, loose or exposed, constantly on, not flashing or out, pole is down or leaning, etc.
picklistOptions.label: School Flashing Beacon Damaged or Missing

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription: Request traffic signal (vehicle, pedestrian, transit & bicycle) information, such as timing reports/cards, programming sheets, drawings, synchro models, analysis reports related to signals.
picklistOptions.label: Traffic Signal Information Request

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription: Request repair of a malfunctioning or damaged traffic signal, signal box, audible crosswalk, or illuminated sign.
picklistOptions.label: Traffic Signal Repair

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription: Report issues with transit signals such as transit signal phase being stuck, on recall, too long, too short or not working; transit detector failure; excessive delays to transit vehicles, Transit Signal Priority (TSP) not working, new TSP request, etc.
picklistOptions.label: Bus/Streetcar (Transit) Signal Issue

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription: Report issues with traffic signals such as signal coordination, excessive vehicle delays, long wait time, access blocking, left-turn/right-turn phase, confusing signal display, congestion due to construction/special events, red light running, etc.
picklistOptions.label: Vehicle Traffic Signal Issue

data.problemTypeName: Report an Encroachment on City Property
data.problemTypeKeywords: boulevard marketing/display, street vending, bikes, outdoor patio, café, orphaned spaces, dumpster, disposal bins, bike lanes, bicycle.

locationDescriptionValidation.regExValidationMessage: Certain special characters are not allowed in location description.

learnMoreArticle.title: Bylaw enforcement - encroachments - on City property
learnMoreArticle.description: Encroachments are defined as anything installed, constructed, or planted within the public road allowance that was not installed, constructed, or planted by the City.

intakeQuestions.regExValidationMessage: Only alphanumeric characters, space, parentheses, strudels, commas, apostrophes, ampersand, forward slash, question mark, hyphens, colon, and periods are allowed.
intakeQuestions.questionText: What is encroaching on City property?
Identify the location of the encroachment

picklistOptions.label: Boulevard (the area between the sidewalk and curb)

eligibilityQuestions.questionText: Is your request related to items constructed, or placed on City property  (boulevards, sidewalks, roads, bicycle lanes) that were not installed or placed by the City?

picklistOptions.label: No
picklistOptions.ineligibleExplanationText: This request is only for reporting encroachments on City property. Search the 311 Knowledge Base for appropriate action for other types of complaints.

eligibilityQuestions.questionText: What has been placed or constructed on City property (boulevard, sidewalk, road or bike lane)?

picklistOptions.label: Dumpster/Disposal/Storage Bin in a Bike Lane
Dumpster/Disposal/Storage Bin without a permit on the road
Parked Vehicle
picklistOptions.ineligibleExplanationText: Parked vehicles are not reported as encroachments. Select the Bylaw Violations and Property Issues category, then Illegal Parking.

picklistOptions.label: Buildings or other permanent structures (i.e. poles, walls, gates, fences and other similar items, or signs, canopies and fire escapes attached to buildings)
Non-permanent structures that are abutting or encroaching on public right of way
Abandoned Bicycles (bikes) on public right of way
picklistOptions.ineligibleExplanationText: To report an abandoned bike on City road allowance, a bike locked to a City tree or displaying a for sale/ advertising sign, see "Abandoned bike".

picklistOptions.label: Display, Boulevard or Marketing
picklistOptions.ineligibleExplanationText: A patio, café, or boulevard marketing on City property requires permits and/or enforcement action Municipal Licensing & Standards.  To submit a service request for bylaw enforcement for patios, cafes or boulevard marketing, see "Business Complaint".

picklistOptions.label: Café on public road, street or boulevard
picklistOptions.ineligibleExplanationText: Please submit a service request "CaféTO Encroachment Complaint"

picklistOptions.label: Café on a sidewalk or immediately in front of or at a side of a building wall of a restaurant
picklistOptions.ineligibleExplanationText: To make a complaint for sidewalk cafés and outdoor patios on City property, please submit a service request "Business Complaint".

picklistOptions.label: Café on private property or parking lot
picklistOptions.ineligibleExplanationText: To make a complaint for Café on private property or parking lot, please submit service request "Zoning Regulations Violations" under property issues.

picklistOptions.label: Street vending or mobile business
picklistOptions.ineligibleExplanationText: To submit a service request for bylaw enforcement for a busker, street vendor, mobile business, or other mobile businesses on City property, see "Public Spaces Complaint".

eligibilityQuestions.questionText: Is the grass that need maintenance or prohibited plant located on private property?

picklistOptions.label: No
picklistOptions.ineligibleExplanationText: This service request is for complaints on long grass or prohibited plants on private property. For long grass on boulevard submit a service request for Long Grass and Weed on the Boulevard

eligibilityQuestions.questionText: Does the property contain prohibited plants or grass more than 20 centimetres (8 inches) in height?

picklistOptions.label: No
picklistOptions.ineligibleExplanationText: Under the by-law, Chapter 489 of the Municipal Code states property owners are required to cut turfgrass (long grass) exceeding 8 inches in height and keep their property free of prohibited plants. Refer to the Knowledge base for list of prohibited plants

eligibilityQuestions.questionText: Who is making the complaint?

picklistOptions.label: Property Owner
picklistOptions.ineligibleExplanationText: The owner is responsible for the maintenance of their own property.

learnMoreArticle.title: Report a construction noise bylaw infraction

intakeQuestions.questionText: What type of construction noise is disturbing you?

picklistOptions.label: Steady impact noises such as a jackhammer
Other (provide any details in the additional information field)

intakeQuestions.questionText: What do you believe is causing the noise?

picklistOptions.label: Small hand tools (drill, saw, pneumatic power tool etc.)
Gas powered equipment (finishing power trowel, water pump)
Other (provide any details in the additional information field)

intakeQuestions.regExValidationMessage: Only alphanumeric characters, space, parentheses, strudels, commas, apostrophes, ampersand, forward slash, question mark, hyphens, colon, and periods are allowed.
intakeQuestions.questionText: Please provide details of the specific area where you are hearing this noise. For example: ‘Backyard’, ‘50m to the left of my address location’, ‘Ground floor’
What time is the construction noise occurring?

picklistOptions.label: Before 7 am or after 7pm on weekdays
Anytime on Sundays or statutory holidays
Before the exemption permit allowable time
After the exemption permit allowable time

intakeQuestions.questionText: How often has the construction noise been disturbing?
What type of construction site?

picklistOptions.label: A residence with the homeowner constructing a project (fence/patio etc)

questions.regExValidationMessage: Only alphanumeric characters, space, parentheses, strudels, commas, apostrophes, ampersand, forward slash, question mark, hyphens, colon, and periods are allowed.
questions.questionText: Provide details below

intakeQuestions.regExValidationMessage: Only alphanumeric characters, space, parentheses, strudels, commas, apostrophes, ampersand, forward slash, question mark, hyphens, colon, and periods are allowed.
intakeQuestions.questionText: How were you able to determine where the construction noise was coming from?
Were you ever made aware whether a Noise Exemption Permit was issued to this operation
If a Municipal Licensing & Standard bylaw officer needs more details about the noise, how would you like them to contact you?
intakeQuestions.helpText: In the next step, please share your phone number or email so a bylaw officer can reach you if needed. If we can’t contact you, we might not be able to follow up on your complaint.

eligibilityQuestions.questionText: Is the construction noise occurring between 7 p.m. to 7 a.m. Monday - Friday or 7 p.m. to 9 a.m. Saturday or all day Sunday and statutory holidays?

picklistOptions.label: No
picklistOptions.ineligibleExplanationText: Construction noise outside of specified period is not a bylaw violation. You may document this noise by using the "Permitted or Exempted Noise" request for data collection purpose.

eligibilityQuestions.helpText: Noise must be occurring from 7 p.m. to 7 a.m. Monday - Friday or 7:00pm to 9 a.m. on Saturdays or all day Sunday and statutory holidays.

eligibilityQuestions.questionText: Is the noise caused by government work due to safety measures, i.e. construction, rehabilitation, or maintenance work that is conducted by the City, Province of Ontario, Government of Canada, or any of its agencies or agents?

picklistOptions.label: Yes
picklistOptions.ineligibleExplanationText: Government work and noise due to safety measures are exempt from the Noise Bylaw. You may document this noise by using the "Permitted or Exempted Noise" request for data collection purpose.

eligibilityQuestions.helpText: Construction must be caused by a vehicle other than a City of Toronto vehicle or vehicle working on its behalf for construction, rehabilitation, or maintenance work.

eligibilityQuestions.questionText: Is the noise caused by any of the following?

picklistOptions.label: Extreme sound levels due to construction operations during permitted hours.
picklistOptions.ineligibleExplanationText: Municipal Licensing & Standard does not respond to this type of noise. You may document this noise by using the "Permitted or Exempted Noise" request for data collection purpose.

picklistOptions.label: Occasional construction noise outside of permitted hours from a residential unit or home where there is no contractor
picklistOptions.ineligibleExplanationText: Municipal Licensing & Standard does not respond to this type of noise. You may document this noise by using the "Permitted or Exempted Noise" request for data collection purpose.

data.description: Report noise from construction activities, equipment, or crane work (including erection, dismantling, or any crane work requiring road closure) outside permitted hours. Examples include noise from building repair, demolition, or excavation.

refinementQuestions.questionText: What type of pick up was missed?

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeName: Multi-Residential Electronic Waste Not Picked Up
picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription:
Report electronic waste not picked up at a multi-residential property.
Note: Report only electronic waste not picked up within 24 hours of the scheduled collection day.
picklistOptions.label: Electronic Waste

questions.questionText: What collection service do you receive?

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeName: Multi-residential Front End Loaded Oversized Items Not Picked Up
picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription: Multi-residential front-end loaded customer reporting furniture or oversized items not picked up.
picklistOptions.label: Front End Waste Collection Customers (e.g. Dumpsters)

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeName: Multi-residential Oversized Items Not Picked Up
picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription:
Report oversized items at a multi-residential property not picked up. The request must be submitted by the superintendent or property manager.
Note: Collection for oversized items will take place on the scheduled furniture collection day.
picklistOptions.label: Daytime Curbside Collection

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeName: Multi-Residential - Furniture Pile Night Collection - Not Picked Up
picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription:
Request missed oversized items during night collection for multi-residential properties. 
Note: Furniture infested with bedbugs, must be wrapped in plastic and sealed with tape.
picklistOptions.label: Nighttime Curbside Collection

questions.questionText: What collection service do you receive?

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeName: Multi-residential Front End Loaded Garbage Not Picked Up
picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription:
Report front-end loaded garbage at a multi-residential property not picked up.
Note: Request must be submitted by the superintendent or property manager.
picklistOptions.label: Front End Waste Collection Bin (e.g. Dumpster)

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeName: Multi-residential Garbage Cart Not Picked Up
picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription:
Report a garbage cart not picked up at a multi-residential property. 
Note: Report only garbage not picked up within 1 business day of the scheduled collection day.
picklistOptions.label: Garbage Bin - Daytime Collection

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeName: Multi-residential Garbage Pile Not Picked Up
picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription:
Report a garbage pile not picked up at a multi-residential property. 
Note: The request must be submitted by the superintendent or property manager.
picklistOptions.label: Garbage Bags - Daytime Curbside Collection

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeName: Multi-Residential - Garbage Pile Night Collection - Not Picked Up
picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription:
Report missed garbage pile, night collection at a multi-residential property.
Note: Request must be submitted by the superintendent or property manager.
picklistOptions.label: Garbage Bags - Nighttime Curbside Collection

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription:
Report missed pick-up (Toxic Taxi) for household hazardous waste (HHW).
Note: Household Hazardous Waste (HHW) pick-up is only available for single-family, RUAC (residential unit above commercial) or a multi-residential dwellings.
picklistOptions.label: Hazardous Waste

questions.questionText: What collection service do you receive?

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeName: Multi-residential Front-end Loaded Organics Not Picked Up
picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription:
Report missed pick-up of organic front-end loader collection at multi-residential property.
Note: Request must be submitted by the superintendent or property manager.
picklistOptions.label: Front End Waste Collection Bin (e.g. Dumpster)

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeName: Multi-residential Organic Cart Not Picked Up
picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription:
Report an organic cart at a multi-residential property not picked up.
Note: Request must be submitted by the superintendent or property manager.
picklistOptions.label: Organic Bin for Front End Collection Customers

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeName: Multi-residential Organic Bin Not Picked Up
picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription:
Report an organic bin not picked up at a multi-residential property.
Note: The request must be submitted by the superintendent or property manager.
picklistOptions.label: Organic Bin for Curbside Collection

questions.questionText: What collection service do you receive?

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeName: Multi-residential Front End Loaded Yard Waste Not Picked up
picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription: Report multi-residential front-end loaded yard waste not picked up.
picklistOptions.label: Front End Waste Collection Customers (e.g. Dumpsters)

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeName: Multi-residential Yard Waste Not Picked Up
picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription:
Report yard waste at multi-residential property not picked up.
Note: Only report yard waste not picked up within 1 business day of the scheduled collection day.
picklistOptions.label: Curbside Collection

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription: Request an emergency water service turn off.
picklistOptions.label: Emergency Water Service Turn Off due to Pipe Leak or Burst

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription: Request for a first-time water service turn-on for a building that is newly built and has a 4-inch or larger water service (e.g. townhouse complexes, multi-story residential, commercial or industrial  buildings).
picklistOptions.label: New Large Water Service Turn-On

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription: Book an appointment to turn off the water service between 8 a.m. to 8 p.m., Monday to Sunday. Book a water service turn-on as well, and the water turn-on fee will be waived if it is within 30 minutes of water turn-off, and the staff is still on-site.
picklistOptions.label: Water  Service Turn Off

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription: Book an appointment to turn on the water service. Appointments are available 24 hours a day, 7 days a week. The water turn-on fee will be waived if it is within 30 minutes of water turn off, and the staff is still on-site.
picklistOptions.label: Water Service Turn-On

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription:
Request inspection of utility cut repair that is loose and/or making noise. Utility cuts can be identified by rectangular asphalt patches on the road or roadside.
Note: Metal plates are often temporarily installed over utility cuts.
picklistOptions.label: Utility Cut - Road

Request inspection of  utility cut repair on the road or roadside. Utility cuts can be identified by rectangular asphalt patches on the road or roadside.
Note: metal plates are often temporarily installed over utility cuts.

learnMoreArticle.title: Report  Unreasonable & Persistent Noise

intakeQuestions.questionText: What makes this noise and/or sound-induced vibration unreasonable?

picklistOptions.label: Vibrations from the noise are very disturbing
Occurs late at night and early morning hours
Other (provide any details in the additional information field)

intakeQuestions.questionText: How persistent is this noise and/or sound-induced vibration?

picklistOptions.label: Occurs continually for 10 minutes in an hour
Occurs intermittently for a total of 10 minutes in an hour
Occurs for a continual period up to a half hour to an hour
Occurs for a continual period one hour or more

intakeQuestions.regExValidationMessage: Only alphanumeric characters, space, parentheses, strudels, commas, apostrophes, ampersand, forward slash, question mark, hyphens, colon, and periods are allowed.
intakeQuestions.questionText: Identify the type of noise and/or sound-induced vibration being heard/felt?
How often is the unreasonable and persistent noise and/or sound-induced  vibration occurring?
Where is the noise source coming from?

questions.questionText: Where is the residential neighbour located?

picklistOptions.label: In the same building envelope (apartment, condominium, semi-detached home)
Outside of your building envelope (next door neighbour)

questions.questionText: If the source is a neighbour in your building complex, have you attempted to contact the Landlord, Condominium Corporation or whatever authority applies to obtain a resolution?

intakeQuestions.regExValidationMessage: Only alphanumeric characters, space, parentheses, strudels, commas, apostrophes, ampersand, forward slash, question mark, hyphens, colon, and periods are allowed.
intakeQuestions.questionText: Please provide details of the specific area where you are hearing this noise. For example: ‘Backyard’, ‘50m to the left of my address location’, ‘Ground floor’

intakeQuestions.regExValidationMessage: Please enter the correct time format HH:MM:SS.000
intakeQuestions.questionText: What time of day is the noise most disturbing?
Have you contacted the individual responsible for the noise?
If a Municipal Licensing & Standard bylaw officer needs more details about the noise, how would you like them to contact you?
intakeQuestions.helpText: In the next step, please share your phone number or email so a bylaw officer can reach you if needed. If we can’t contact you, we might not be able to follow up on your complaint.

eligibilityQuestions.questionText: Is the noise unwanted, excessive, persistent and long in duration (more than ten minutes) or intermittently over a period of one hour or more?

picklistOptions.label: No
picklistOptions.ineligibleExplanationText: To learn more about the Noise Bylaw, refer to 311 Knowledge Base.

eligibilityQuestions.questionText: Is this a complaint about a person or group's behaviour? (i.e. aggressive, disorderly, violent, or a risk to public safety)

picklistOptions.label: Yes
picklistOptions.ineligibleExplanationText: To report any of these types of noises, call the Toronto Police Service non-emergency number. If it is an emergency, call 9-1-1.

eligibilityQuestions.questionText: Is the noise caused by an animal, amplified device, Instrument sound, construction, power device, stationary machines (e.g. air conditioners, generators), loading or unloading or a motor vehicle?

picklistOptions.label: Yes
picklistOptions.ineligibleExplanationText: To initiate a request for amplified sound, construction, power device, stationary source, loading or unloading, or a motor vehicle noise complaint, please refer to the Knowledge Base for Noise related service requests.

eligibilityQuestions.questionText: Is this a complaint about commonplace household or workplace sounds such as furniture being moved, children playing or people engaged in conversation?

picklistOptions.label: Yes
picklistOptions.ineligibleExplanationText: Noise such as furniture being moved, children playing, or people engaged in conversation are not enforceable by Municipal Licensing & Standard. You may document this noise by using the "Permitted or Exempted Noise" request for data collection purpose.

learnMoreArticle.title: Report Animal Noise

intakeQuestions.questionText: Indicate animal type

intakeQuestions.regExValidationMessage: Only alphanumeric characters, space, parentheses, strudels, commas, apostrophes, ampersand, forward slash, question mark, hyphens, colon, and periods are allowed.
intakeQuestions.questionText: Details of the complaint

intakeQuestions.regExValidationMessage: Only alphanumeric characters, space, parentheses, strudels, commas, apostrophes, ampersand, forward slash, question mark, hyphens, colon, and periods are allowed.
intakeQuestions.questionText: Indicate times of day when this is occurring

eligibilityQuestions.questionText: Is the unwanted animal noise excessive persistent and long in duration (not a one-time/occasional occurrence)?

picklistOptions.label: No
picklistOptions.ineligibleExplanationText: Animal Services only investigates persistent barking, calling or whining or other similar persistent noise-making by any animal kept or used for any purpose is prohibited at all times.

eligibilityQuestions.questionText: Are you able to provide the address of where the animal resides (not an intersection, school yard, or park)?

picklistOptions.label: No
picklistOptions.ineligibleExplanationText: City staff requires the address of where the dog resides to conduct this type of investigation request.

picklistOptions.label: Yes

eligibilityQuestions.questionText: Is the work scheduled for more than 2 weeks from today?

picklistOptions.label: No
picklistOptions.ineligibleExplanationText: The response time for someone to check the Water Service Box is 10 business days. Please submit your service request at least 2 weeks prior to your scheduled work.

data.description:
Request for Toronto Water to check or repair a water service box on the sidewalk or front lawn. 
Note: A water service box is a round brown valve on the sidewalk or front lawn. Service boxes give the City access to shut off water to your home.

locationDescriptionValidation.regExValidationMessage: Certain special characters are not allowed in location description.

learnMoreArticle.title: Report of no water

intakeQuestions.questionText: Select Property Type
If this is a commercial, multi-story residential or townhouse complex,  is this a large service (more than 4 inches)?
Are you the homeowner/ property owner, manager, superintendent or contractor?

intakeQuestions.regExValidationMessage: Only alphanumeric characters, space, parentheses, strudels, commas, apostrophes, ampersand, forward slash, question mark, hyphens, colon, and periods are allowed.
intakeQuestions.questionText: Property Owner / Manager Name

intakeQuestions.regExValidationMessage: Only numeric characters are allowed.
intakeQuestions.questionText: Property Owner / Manager Phone Number
Are all the taps affected?
Have you checked the aerators/faucet filters for any blockages and have you checked that the stop/waste valve in the basement (at water meter) is fully open
Have you had any work done recently by a plumber?
Is there a temporary water main or service outside your home (blue hose connected to your home in the street)?
Are there any dog/s at your residence?
intakeQuestions.helpText: Please ensure that your dog/s are under your care and control when Toronto Water staff attend your residence.

eligibilityQuestions.questionText: Is there a watermain break or construction work in the area? (you can check the City's No Water Map for more information)

picklistOptions.label: Yes
picklistOptions.ineligibleExplanationText: Your water should be turned back on after the watermain break is fixed or construction work is completed. If there is no water after the fix, please call 311.

eligibilityQuestions.questionText: If this is happening during cold weather, have you checked whether the water pipes are frozen?

picklistOptions.label: I haven't checked whether the pipes are frozen or not
picklistOptions.ineligibleExplanationText:
For tips on how to thaw frozen pipes in your home please see  http://www.toronto.ca/frozenpipes 
and contact 311 if the pipes are not frozen.

eligibilityQuestions.questionText: Is this request from the property owner, or superintendent, property, facility or maintenance manager of the building?

picklistOptions.label: No
picklistOptions.ineligibleExplanationText: Please contact your landlord or property owner. If your landlord or property owner hasn't taken any actions within a reasonable time to rectify the issue, please submit a service request "No Water, Gas or Power".

eligibilityQuestions.questionText: Are all the taps affected?

picklistOptions.label: No
picklistOptions.ineligibleExplanationText: If all the taps are not affected, please contact a plumber

eligibilityQuestions.questionText: Have you checked the aerators for any blockages and have you checked that the stop/waste valve in the basement (at water meter) is fully open?

picklistOptions.label: No
picklistOptions.ineligibleExplanationText: Please check the aerators for blockages and check the stop/waste valve to ensure it is fully open.

data.description: Report no water service at your location.

refinementQuestions.questionText: What is the issue that you are reporting?

questions.questionText: Is this a request to investigate an aggressive dog that is owned or stray?

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription: Report an aggressive owned dog (e.g., not stray) that is exhibiting behaviours that pose a menace to the safety of persons or domestic animals.
picklistOptions.label: Owned Dog

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription: Report an aggressive stray dog exhibiting behaviours that pose a menace to the safety of persons or domestic animals.
picklistOptions.label: Stray Dog

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription: Report an animal owner who fails to provide adequate care for their animals. Adequate and appropriate care includes care sufficient to preserve the health and well-being of an animal (for example not providing food or water).
picklistOptions.label: Animal Care Complaint

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription: Report a dog owner who fails to provide adequate care for the dog or the dog is in a potentially serious state, e.g. starvation, dehydration, inadequate shelter, etc.
picklistOptions.label: Animal kept outside in extreme weather

questions.questionText: What is the issue with the dog?

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription: Report a dog who is kept on a chain, rope or similar restraining device of less than two (2) meters in length, with restricted movement and suffering. The dog shall not be tethered unsupervised for longer than one hour.
picklistOptions.label: Short Leash or Tether

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription:
Report a dog that is being tied and left unsupervised from its owner for longer than one hour. 
Note: If animal cruelty is involved, Toronto Animal Services will contact Toronto Police.
picklistOptions.label: Tied for more than one hour

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription:
Report a person who is keeping a dog on a choke collar, choke chain, pronged collar or any similar device that is harmful to the animal.
Note: If animal cruelty is involved, Toronto Animal Services will contact Toronto Police directly.
picklistOptions.label: Wearing Prohibited Collar

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription: Report a commercial dog walker who is walking more than six dogs in a City park, green space, waterfront area without the permit visibly displayed.
picklistOptions.label: Commercial dog walker with more than 6 dogs and/or without licence in a park

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription: Report incidents of dogs harassing or chasing coyotes.
picklistOptions.label: Dog harassing/chasing coyote

questions.questionText: What is the complaint about?

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription: Report an owner whose dog is off-leash outside of a designated off-leash area.
picklistOptions.label: Dog being walked without a leash

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription: Report a dog allowed to frequently run at large or allowed to roam (owner not present). All dogs must be in care and control of a person and on a leash (unless in designated off-leash areas).
picklistOptions.label: Dog frequently roaming

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeName: Dog Off-Leash in a City Park
picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription: Report a dog off-leash and not under the care or control of a person in a City park outside of a designated off-leash area.
picklistOptions.label: Dog off-leash in a park with the owner

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription: Report a stray domestic animal off the premises of the owner and not in care and control of a person. Toronto Animal Services requests if possible to confine the animal or remain with the animal until an officer arrives.
picklistOptions.label: Dog off-leash in a park without the owner

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription: Report dog off-leash incidents on school property.
picklistOptions.label: Dog off-leash on a school property

questions.questionText: What is the complaint about?

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription: Report a dog owner with a dangerous dog designation that fails to muzzle the dog.
picklistOptions.label: Dangerous Dog without Muzzle

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeName: Investigate - Dangerous Dog in Off-Leash Area
picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription: Report a dog owner who allows a dog with a dangerous dog designation to  use public off-leash areas
picklistOptions.label: Dangerous Dog in Off-Leash Area

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeName: Investigate - Dangerous Dog with no Warning Sign Displayed
picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription: Report a dog owner who fails to display the required dangerous dog warning sign on their property
picklistOptions.label: Dangerous Dog with no Warning Sign Displayed

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeName: Investigate - Dangerous Dog with no Dangerous Dog Tag
picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription: Report a dog owner who fails to put the required dangerous dog tag on their dog
picklistOptions.label: Dangerous Dog with no Dangerous Dog Tag
Dogs with a dangerous dog designation

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription: Report a domestic animal (except a cat) that is kept outside for any continuous period exceeding thirty (30) minutes without a weather-proofed and insulated enclosure/shelter.
picklistOptions.label: Inappropriate or No Shelter

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription:
Report someone who has more than dogs three (3) or cats six (6) or rabbits (4) or guinea pigs (4) in and about a dwelling unit.
Note: This does not apply to individual members of a rescue group, registered with Toronto Animal Services.
picklistOptions.label: Keeping more than 3 dogs

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription: Report a domestic animal on private property that is living in unsanitary conditions (accumulation of animal feces or strong odour).
picklistOptions.label: Odour or Unsanitary Conditions

questions.questionText: Where is the issue?

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription: Report a dog owner or a dog walker failing to pick up dog excrement in a City of Toronto park.
picklistOptions.label: City Park

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription:
Report a dog owner or walker who fails to pick up or remove excrement from public or private property. The address of the dog owner must be provided to complete this request.
Note: For multi-residential property speak with the Property Manager.
picklistOptions.label: Public or Private Property
Owner Fails to Remove Dog Excrement

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription: Report a Pit Bull or non-compliance of the Dog Owners Liability Act (DOLA) by the owner.
picklistOptions.label: Pit Bull

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription:
Report a person who is using a prohibited collar (choke chain/collar) or keeping a dog on a rope/similar restraining device of less than three (3 meters) in length.
Note: If animal cruelty is involved, Toronto Animal Services will contact Toronto Police.
picklistOptions.label: Prohibited Restraints

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription: Submit a service request for an investigation into an unlicensed cat or dog. All dogs and cats owned in Toronto must be licensed and wear a tag.
picklistOptions.label: Unlicensed dog

picklistOptions.refinementProblemTypeDescription:
Report a person who is walking more than three dogs anywhere in the City without a commercial dog walker's permit. The permit must be prominently displayed.
Note: Commercial dog walkers are allowed to walk 4-6 dogs at a time.
picklistOptions.label: Walking more than 3 dogs on City property

intakeQuestions.questionText: If Other, please specify

eligibilityQuestions.questionText: Is this a request to maintain a City-owned tree on the road allowance or in a City park, but not a tree growing in a hard surface (such as concrete planter or paver and growing in front of a commercial property)?

picklistOptions.label: No
picklistOptions.ineligibleExplanationText: This service request is only used for non-commercial tree maintenance requests. Please select the appropriate request for a commercial tree from the list of available service requests.

eligibilityQuestions.questionText: Is this request regarding brush pick up that has been left behind by Toronto Hydro?

picklistOptions.label: Yes
picklistOptions.ineligibleExplanationText: Please contact Toronto Hydro to request brush pick up.

eligibilityQuestions.questionText: What type of tree maintenance request is this?

picklistOptions.label: Brush clean up from tree maintenance that occurred more than 10 days ago
Remove tree stakes or ties from a tree planted more than 2 years ago
Repair of tree stakes or ties in a tree planted less than 2 years ago
Watering request for a tree that was planted within the last 2 years
Tree roots above the ground causing a significant trip hazard
Asian Long Horned Beetle, Emerald Ash Borer or European Starlings
picklistOptions.ineligibleExplanationText: Submit a service request for Integrated Pest Management Inspection.

picklistOptions.label: Bees or Wasps
picklistOptions.ineligibleExplanationText: Bee or wasp nests in City‑owned trees are managed by the City only during the active season (June to October) when the nest poses a public safety risk. During this time, service requests may be submitted under Pest Complaints.

picklistOptions.label: Other maintenance issue
picklistOptions.ineligibleExplanationText: The City of Toronto only investigates the issues listed for this service request.  Please review other available service requests to see if there is a more appropriate request type for the issue you are concerned about.

learnMoreArticle.title: Dangerous Private Trees

intakeQuestions.questionText: Is the private tree an immediate safety hazard to people or property? (i.e. large branch cracked, detached, split, dangling or cracked trunk OR is tree likely to fall within 24 hours)
What is the concern?

picklistOptions.label: Broken and Hanging Branch in Tree
Tree is Causing Damage to Property

intakeQuestions.regExValidationMessage: Only alphanumeric characters, space, parentheses, strudels, commas, apostrophes, ampersand, forward slash, question mark, hyphens, colon, and periods are allowed.
intakeQuestions.questionText: Please describe the concern (e.g. condition of tree/limb)

intakeQuestions.regExValidationMessage: Only alphanumeric characters, space, parentheses, strudels, commas, apostrophes, ampersand, forward slash, question mark, hyphens, colon, and periods are allowed.
intakeQuestions.questionText: Please describe the size of the hazardous tree or branch (e.g. approximate diameter and height)
Where on the property is the tree located?

picklistOptions.label: Front Yard of a Private Property
Side Yard of a Private Property
Rear Yard of a Private Property
Other Part of a Private Property

intakeQuestions.regExValidationMessage: Only alphanumeric characters, space, parentheses, strudels, commas, apostrophes, ampersand, forward slash, question mark, hyphens, colon, and periods are allowed.
intakeQuestions.questionText: Where on the tree is the problem located? (ex. In the upper branches, on the trunk, at the bottom)
If SR is created Mon-Fri after 2 p.m. or on weekends or Statutory Holidays, can the concern be attended to on the next business day?
Is the branch obstructing the road or pedestrian traffic?
Is the branch obstructing Driveway, Public Laneway, Public Walkway?

questions.regExValidationMessage: Only alphanumeric characters, space, parentheses, strudels, commas, apostrophes, ampersand, forward slash, question mark, hyphens, colon, and periods are allowed.
questions.questionText: If applicable, describe the details of the obstruction.

eligibilityQuestions.questionText: Is the tree on Private Property?

picklistOptions.label: No
picklistOptions.ineligibleExplanationText: If the tree is on City property and an immediate hazard, please submit a service request for ‘Tree Emergency Clean-Up’. If the tree is not an immediate hazard, please review service requests under 'Tree Planting & Maintenance'.

eligibilityQuestions.questionText: Are you the Property Owner?

picklistOptions.label: Yes
picklistOptions.ineligibleExplanationText: Maintenance of privately owned trees is the responsibility of the property owner.

eligibilityQuestions.questionText: Is the private tree an immediate hazard (A tree, or a limb or branch of the tree that is decayed, damaged or dangerous; tree or branch that poses a danger to persons or property)?

picklistOptions.label: No
picklistOptions.ineligibleExplanationText: City of Toronto only maintains City trees. For trees on private property, the owner of the tree is required to maintain the tree; concerns about the general maintenance of a private tree must be addressed with the owner.

learnMoreArticle.description: Sewer and water service cards typically include information about the water and sewer connections going to a property.

learnMoreArticle.title: Stray healthy domestic animal - pick-up request

intakeQuestions.questionText: Are you reporting this between 1:00 a.m. - 6 a.m.?
intakeQuestions.helpText: Toronto Animal Services staff are unavailable from 1:00 to 6:00 a.m. Service requests submitted during this time will be reviewed after 6:00 a.m.

intakeQuestions.questionText: Indicate animal Type

intakeQuestions.regExValidationMessage: Only alphanumeric characters, space, parentheses, strudels, commas, apostrophes, ampersand, forward slash, question mark, hyphens, colon, and periods are allowed.
intakeQuestions.questionText: Description of the animal
Are you willing to stay onsite until the officer arrives?

eligibilityQuestions.questionText: Is this request to pick-up a confined stray domestic animal?

picklistOptions.label: No
picklistOptions.ineligibleExplanationText: This service request is for pick-up of a stray healthy domestic animal. For other animal related matters, please see the knowledge base to determine the appropriate service request.

eligibilityQuestions.questionText: If the confined animal is a dog, did the dog attack or bite any human or animals?

picklistOptions.label: Yes
picklistOptions.ineligibleExplanationText: Please see "Stray - Attack".

data.description: Request to pick-up a stray healthy domestic animal. The customer is willing to stay onsite with the animal until the officer arrives, if possible.

locationDescriptionValidation.regExValidationMessage: Certain special characters are not allowed in location description.

learnMoreArticle.title: Sidewalk is damaged or broken

intakeQuestions.questionText: Is the damage a potential safety hazard to pedestrian or cyclists (i.e. broken and posing a trip hazard)?

questions.questionText: Is the surrounding area cordoned off with cones or barrels; or marked with orange paint?

intakeQuestions.questionText: Please describe the damage to the sidewalk

questions.regExValidationMessage: Only alphanumeric characters, space, parentheses, strudels, commas, apostrophes, ampersand, forward slash, question mark, hyphens, colon, and periods are allowed.
questions.questionText: If other, please describe

intakeQuestions.questionText: Is this an ongoing issue/ Have you noticed this issue before?

eligibilityQuestions.questionText: Is this issue located on Toronto Island?

picklistOptions.label: Yes
picklistOptions.ineligibleExplanationText: Maintenance issues on Toronto Island need to be forwarded Parks staff for investigation. Please call 311 to be transferred to the park supervisor responsible for Toronto Island.

eligibilityQuestions.questionText: Is this a request for a road or sidewalk maintenance issue (potholes, sidewalk or road repair) along Eglinton Avenue between Kennedy Road and Black Creek Drive within a work zone?

picklistOptions.label: Yes
picklistOptions.ineligibleExplanationText: Metrolinx is responsible for work zone issues for the Eglinton Crosstown LRT. Please contact the ECLRT Call Centre.

eligibilityQuestions.questionText: What type of sidewalk is it?

picklistOptions.label: Brick, interlock or stone
picklistOptions.ineligibleExplanationText: For sidewalk that is made of bricks, interlocks or stones, please submit service request "Damaged Brick, Interlock or Stone Sidewalk".

data.articleContent:
Description of Service/Summary:

Potholes form when water seeps into cracks in the road, freezes, and expands, causing the pavement to break apart. They are especially common in spring due to repeated freeze‑thaw cycles. Residents can help keep roads safe by reporting potholes to 311 for timely repair.

Potholes Road Surface Damage Criteria:

Pothole size thresholds vary depending on the road classification, helping crews determine when repairs are required and how to prioritize work for safety and efficiency. The required minimum measurements for pothole repair on each road type are:

  • Expressways: over 600 cm² in surface area and 8 cm deep
  • Arterial Roads: over 800 cm² in surface area and 8 cm deep
  • Collector and Local Roads: over 1,000 cm² in surface area and 8 cm deep 

What Causes Potholes:

Potholes are created when water penetrates the top layer of asphalt through cracks in the road. After the moisture freezes and expands, sections of the pavement are forced up. The weight of vehicles going over this section of road breaks the pavement and the asphalt is forced out. Potholes are more frequent in the spring, after the freeze/thaw action following winter.

Steps to Fix a Pothole: 

To combat the problem, the City of Toronto's Transportation Division has a number of work crews that are assigned to the job of fixing potholes and similar road defects as close to year-round as the weather permits. 

  • Crews place asphalt and rake it into the pothole. 
  • The asphalt is tamped down and smoothed out until the road surface is improved
  • During winter months potholes are temporarily patched with cold mix asphalt to make the road safe.
  • More permanent repairs are performed with hot asphalt when warmer conditions prevail.

How to Report a Pothole Road Damage Request:

To report potholes on City of Toronto roads, bike lanes or expressways, please  submit a service request online at: toronto.ca/311 by selecting the “Roads, Sidewalks, Bicycle & Traffic Safety” Service Request category or call 311. 

City of Toronto Expressways include:

The following Expressways are maintained by the City: 

  • Don Valley Parkway (DVP) - south of the 401
  • Highway 27 - north from Highway 401 west of Martin Grove Rd., to Steeles Ave.
  • Gardiner Expressway
  • Hwy 2a (Kingston Rd.)
  • Allen Expressway
  • Black Creek Dr.

If you wish to report potholes on a Provincial Highway or ramp, such as the 400,401, 403, 404, 409, 410, 427, or QEW,  visit Potholes - provincial highways.

What Happens When a Request is Created:

  • The City will review your request and assess the pothole.
  • Repairs are prioritized based on road type and safety risk.
  • If you signed up for updates, we’ll notify you when work begins.

Expected Timelines for Repair: 

The Estimated Resolution Timeframe is an average time for pothole repairs across all road classifications. The timelines below outline the expected repair periods for each road type, all of which meet or exceed provincial maintenance standards.  Highly frequented roads are attended to more quickly:
  • Emergency Repair: Within 24 hours (for major roadways where the pothole is large and poses risk to vehicles or pedestrians)
  • City Expressways (over 40,000 vehicles per day, e.g., DVP, Allen): 4 days
  • Arterial Roads (over 8,000 vehicles per day, e.g. Yonge St.): 4 days
  • Collector Road (2,500 to 8,000 vehicles per day, e.g., John St.): 14 days
  • Local Street (Less than 2,500 vehicles per day, e.g., Mercer St.): 21 days
  • Public Laneways: 21 days
If a pothole requires more extensive work, crews will complete a temporary repair within the timelines listed above, followed by a permanent repair at a later date.
 
Information on the number of potholes filled, the more detailed process of fixing potholes, and the cost of this work can be found by visiting the City of Toronto website.
 

Additional Information and Facts on Potholes in the City of Toronto:

  • The City has a comprehensive pothole repair program to maintain safe and reliable roadways for all users.
  • Toronto is a big city with a huge network of roads, bikeways and surface transit options, and the City is committed to keeping our road network safe for all residents and visitors.
  • Potholes are created when water penetrates the top layer of asphalt through cracks in the road. After the moisture freezes and expands, sections of the pavement are forced up and the weight of vehicles going over this section of road breaks the pavement, creating a pothole.
  • A winter with more freeze-thaw cycles also increases the number of potholes on city streets.
  • Pothole repair crews also handle other road maintenance activities such as snow clearing and removal, street sweeping and other maintenance and roadway safety work.
  • The public is asked to be safe by respecting work zones and giving crews space while they make repairs.

Submitting a Claim to the City:

If you believe that the City has been negligent in its maintenance of City facilities, roads, trees and sewers, which has caused bodily injury or damage to your property, you can submit a claim to the City of Toronto.
 
For certain types of claims, you are required to submit notice of your claim within 10 days of the date of the incident.
